text.title: django-pytest-mocking Procedure
text.summary: Five steps to a validated mocking spec.

step.name: enumerate-boundaries
step.action: List every external call: HTTP, SDK (Stripe/Twilio), datetime-dependent logic, Celery task triggers.

step.name: pick-technique
step.input: Per boundary: how is the call made?
step.action: HTTP via requests → "responses". SDK / Python import → "unittest-mock-patch". datetime.now / timezone.now → "freezegun" or "time-machine". Celery .delay() → "celery-delay-patch" (verify trigger) or "celery-eager" (verify side effect).

step.name: declare-patch-target
step.input: Per unittest-mock-patch boundary: where is the symbol used?
step.action: patch_target MUST be the CONSUMER's import path (apps.billing.services.stripe.Charge.create), NEVER the definition (stripe.Charge.create).
step.decision-gate: Reject patch_target not starting with the consumer module's import path.

step.name: declare-assertions
step.input: The contract the call must satisfy.
step.action: For each boundary: write at least one assertion that VERIFIES THE CALL happened (assert_called_once_with / assert_called / responses.calls length). Return-value-only checks are insufficient.

step.name: declare-anti-mocks-validate
step.action: anti_mocks MUST include "django-orm" and "time-sleep". Emit spec; run validator.
